verses.1: God, why have you rejected us forever?
verses.2: Remember your congregation, which you purchased of old,
verses.3: Lift up your feet to the perpetual ruins,
verses.4: Your adversaries have roared in the middle of your assembly.
verses.5: They behaved like men wielding axes,
verses.6: Now they break all its carved work down with hatchet and hammers.
verses.7: They have burned your sanctuary to the ground.
verses.8: They said in their heart, “We will crush them completely.”
verses.10: How long, God, shall the adversary reproach?
verses.11: Why do you draw back your hand, even your right hand?
verses.12: Yet God is my King of old,
verses.13: You divided the sea by your strength.
verses.14: You broke the heads of Leviathan in pieces.
verses.15: You opened up spring and stream.
verses.16: The day is yours, the night is also yours.
verses.17: You have set all the boundaries of the earth.
verses.18: Remember this, that the enemy has mocked you, LORD.
verses.19: Don’t deliver the soul of your dove to wild beasts.
verses.21: Don’t let the oppressed return ashamed.
verses.22: Arise, God! Plead your own cause.
verses.23: Don’t forget the voice of your adversaries.
